Bus Timings

Bus 1 in Sharjah: Complete Route and Stops Guide

November 20, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus 1 is a key public transport service in Sharjah, connecting the bustling Sahara Centre Terminal with the Al Rolla Terminal. This route serves a variety of residential, commercial, and recreational areas, making it an essential link for daily commuters and visitors. With its strategic stops across major intersections and key locations in Sharjah, Bus 1 offers a convenient and reliable means of transportation throughout the day.

Route Name 1
Route Description Sahara Centre Terminal - Al Rolla Terminal
Total Distance Approximately 13-15 kilometers
Total Journey Time Approximately 30-60 minutes
Operational Hours 06:00 to 23:00 (Depending on Starting Point)
Fare AED 8 (Cash) / AED 6 (Sayer Card)
Official Website SRTA Gov

Bus Timings

Bus 1 operates with a consistent schedule throughout the day, ensuring frequent service. Here are the departure times:

  • From Al Rolla Terminal: 06:00, 06:18, 06:36, 06:54, 07:12, 07:30, 07:48, 08:06, 08:24, 08:42, 09:00, 09:18, 09:36, 09:54, 10:12, 10:30, 11:00, 11:30, 12:00, 12:30, 13:00, 13:30, 14:00, 14:30, 15:00, 15:30, 16:00, 16:18, 16:36, 16:54, 17:12, 17:30, 17:48, 18:06, 18:24, 18:42, 19:00, 19:18, 19:36, 19:54, 20:12, 20:30, 21:00, 21:30, 21:54, 22:18, 22:42, 23:00

Bus Ticket Price

The fare for Bus 1 aligns with Sharjah’s standard public transportation rates, ensuring affordability for all passengers. Tickets cost AED 8 when paid with cash or AED 6 with a Sayer card. The Sayer card provides a discounted fare, making it a cost-effective choice for frequent travelers and those who prefer a cashless payment method. Overall, Bus 1 offers a convenient and budget-friendly option for daily commuting.

Bus Booking

Bus 1 operates on a first-come, first-served basis, and there is no online booking system for individual seats. These apps provide essential information on bus schedules, route details, and estimated arrival times, ensuring a smooth travel experience. For real-time updates and accurate route planning, downloading the RTA Sharjah app is recommended.

Bus Route

The Bus 1 route provides comprehensive coverage from Sahara Centre Terminal to Al Rolla Terminal. Key stops along the way include:

  1. Sahara Centre Terminal

  2. Al Nahda Street, Sahara Bridge

  3. First Industrial Street, NBF

  4. First Industrial Street, Al Khan Intersection

  5. First Industrial Street, ADNOC Petrol Station

  6. First Industrial Street

  7. First Industrial Street, 64/72 Intersection

  8. First Industrial Street, 70/84 Intersection

  9. King Faisal Road, Maza Signal Junction

  10. King Faisal Road, Bridge

  11. Abu Shagara (Jumbo-Sony)

  12. Al Estiqlal Street, King Faisal Intersection

  13. Al Estiqlal Street, Al Bustan Hotel

  14. Sharjah Naturalization and Residency

  15. Emirates Islamic Bank

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Al Aroba Street, Gold Souq

  2. Panasonic Tower

  3. ADCB Bank

  4. Burj 2000

  5. Geant Supermarket

  6. Al Rolla Terminal

Bus 1 Station Overview

Sahara Centre Terminal

Sahara Centre Terminal is a well-known bus station located adjacent to the popular Sahara Centre shopping mall in Sharjah.

The terminal is equipped with basic facilities, and its strategic location near the Dubai-Sharjah border makes it a convenient stop for those traveling between the two emirates. With various bus routes connecting through this terminal, it ensures smooth transit for passengers heading to different parts of Sharjah and Dubai.

Al Rolla Terminal

Al Rolla Terminal is one of Sharjah’s busiest bus stations, located in the heart of the city. This terminal is a major transit hub that connects various bus routes, making it a central point for commuters traveling within Sharjah and to other emirates.

The terminal is well-equipped with amenities such as seating areas, ticket counters, and nearby shops, providing a comfortable waiting environment for passengers. Its location in a densely populated area ensures that it serves a large number of daily commuters, making it a vital part of Sharjah’s public transport network.

Tourist Destination Along the Way

Bus 1 in Sharjah travels through several notable areas that offer various tourist attractions and destinations:

  1. Sahara Centre Terminal: A major shopping and entertainment destination featuring a wide range of shops, dining options, and a cinema.

2.

  1. Abu Shagara (Jumbo-Sony): A busy commercial area with shops and eateries.

  2. Sharjah Naturalization and Residency: While primarily a government building, it’s located in a bustling area with various amenities.

  3. Al Aroba Street, Gold Souq: This is one of Sharjah’s famous gold souks, where visitors can explore numerous shops selling gold and jewelry.

  4. Panasonic Tower: Situated in a commercial area, it is close to various businesses and dining options.

7.

Each of these locations offers something unique, from shopping and dining to exploring local markets, making Bus 1 a convenient route for accessing some of Sharjah’s key attractions.

Travelling with Careem

Using the Careem app, you can travel to Al Rolla Terminal, the endpoint of Bus 1, starting from Sahara Centre Terminal. The fare for this journey ranges between AED 31 and AED 37. Enjoy a smooth and efficient ride with real-time updates on the app.

Bus 1 in Sharjah provides reliable service between Sahara Centre Terminal and Al Rolla Terminal, covering key residential and commercial areas. With a consistent schedule and affordable fares, it caters well to both daily commuters and occasional travelers. Passengers can use the Sayer card for discounts, though online booking isn’t available. Real-time updates via the RTA Sharjah app help with journey planning.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: November 20, 2025.
